paper.title: An absence of binary companions to Wolf-Rayet stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud: implications for mass loss and black hole masses at low metallicity

extraction.summary: This paper studies Wolf-Rayet stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC), not the Milky Way. While SMC AB1 and SMC AB11 are identified as runaway stars with relative velocities of ~80 km/s, the paper treats them as runaway stars within the SMC (likely post-supernova runaways), not as Galactic-unbound or escaping candidates. No objects are discussed as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The seven apparently single Wolf-Rayet stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud studied in this paper.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These objects are located in the Small Magellanic Cloud (~62 kpc away), not in the Milky Way. The paper does not present them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Abstract describing the study of seven apparently single WR stars in the SMC.

candidate_groups_considered.description: SMC AB1 and SMC AB11, identified by the paper as runaway stars with relative velocities of ~68-91 km/s in the line of sight and ~78-107 km/s transverse.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper identifies these as runaway stars within the SMC (likely post-supernova runaways), but does not discuss them as possibly unbound from the Milky Way Galactic potential. They are ordinary runaways, not HVS or Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Paper identifies SMC AB11 and SMC AB1 as runaway stars based on radial velocity and proper motion differences from their surroundings.
Conclusions restating that SMC AB1 and SMC AB11 appear to be runaway stars with relative line-of-sight velocities of ~80 km/s.
